meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Scaling Issues
content.seo_description: Learn to implement robust analytics to overcome scaling bottlenecks. An intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio projects.
content.answer_block: To scale your projects, you must stop guessing and start measuring. By integrating a lightweight analytics event-tracking layer, you gain visibility into user drop-offs and performance bottlenecks. Use Claude Code to instrument your core workflows, allowing you to iterate based on data rather than intuition, ensuring your SaaS scales effectively.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding your first SaaS, only to realize the growth curve is flat. The frustration isn't in your code quality; it's in your blindness. Without data, you are sailing a ship in the fog, unable to tell which features users love and which ones are causing them to churn.

True engineering isn't just about features; it's about feedback loops. By implementing a professional-grade analytics layer, you transform your project from a static portfolio piece into a data-driven machine. Let’s bridge the gap between 'student project' and 'scalable platform' by mastering the art of event-driven telemetry.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Identify the key user actions that signify value, such as sign-up completion or core feature usage. We need to map these to events before coding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase schema and suggest 5 high-impact events for tracking user retention and feature adoption.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the src/components folder and identify critical user interaction points for tracking.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Integrate a provider like PostHog or Mixpanel to handle the heavy lifting of event ingestion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable analytics wrapper utility in TypeScript that handles event batching and error boundaries.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Install posthog-js and create a hook to initialize the provider in the application root.'

steps.title: Instrument the Critical Path
steps.description: Add tracking events to your authentication flow and primary dashboard interactions to capture user journey data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a HOC or Hook that automatically tracks page views and button clicks with custom metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the analytics hook to all primary navigation links and submit buttons in the dashboard.'

steps.title: Implement Performance Telemetry
steps.description: Capture latency data for your API calls to detect if scaling bottlenecks are client-side or server-side.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a middleware for my API client that measures request duration and logs it as an analytics event.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update the axios client to intercept requests and track latency as a custom metric.'

steps.title: Validate the Data Pipeline
steps.description: Ensure your events are firing correctly in the development environment before pushing to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test script that simulates user interactions and verifies that events are being sent to the analytics backend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a cypress test to confirm that sign-up events trigger correctly in the analytics dashboard.'

pro_tips: Never track everything; focus on 3-5 'North Star' metrics to avoid data noise that hides real insights.
Always sanitize your event payloads to ensure PII (Personally Identifiable Information) never hits your analytics server.
Use distinct user IDs from your database rather than transient session IDs to track long-term user behavior.
Implement 'feature flags' alongside analytics so you can measure the impact of new updates on specific cohorts.
Set up automated alerts in your analytics tool for sudden drops in conversion; don't wait to check the dashboard manually.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 48 hours collecting data. Review the 'User Journey' map and identify the one screen where most users drop off. Use that data to run an A/B test on that specific component.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a data-informed builder. By mastering these telemetry patterns, you've leveled up your architectural thinking. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
